title: linum usitatissimum subsp. angustifolium
intro: Linum usitatissimum subsp. angustifolium, also known as the narrow-leaved flax or linseed, is a subspecies of the flax plant, belonging to the family Linaceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of linseed are numerous, including reducing inflammation and pain, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. Linseed is often prepared into herbal supplements, oil, and tea, and is also used as a food ingredient in baked goods and other products.
